steps.title: Read the vertex from the problem
steps.content: A maximum height of 203 feet occurring at $t = 2$ seconds means the parabola has its vertex at $(h, k) = (2, 203)$. Since this is a maximum, the parabola opens downward, so the leading coefficient $a$ is negative.

steps.title: Write vertex form
steps.content: Vertex form of a quadratic is $f(t) = a(t - h)^2 + k$. Substituting $h = 2$ and $k = 203$ gives $f(t) = a(t - 2)^2 + 203$.

steps.title: Use the launch height to solve for $a$
Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) $-16(t + 203)^2 - 2$ has vertex $(-203, -2)$ — wrong vertex. Eliminate.
  • B) $-16(t - 203)^2 + 2$ has vertex $(203, 2)$ — wrong vertex. Eliminate.
  • C) $-16(t + 2)^2 - 203$ has vertex $(-2, -203)$ — wrong vertex. Eliminate.
  • D) $-16(t - 2)^2 + 203$ has vertex $(2, 203)$ and gives $f(0) = -64 + 203 = 139$. Keep.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
